summary: The smallest coherent slice was a separate benchmark runner instead of forcing the existing Bun-vs-native backend runner to absorb a second comparison axis. Using a dedicated compiled schema fixture kept the benchmark honest and easy to regression-test.
ergonomics: The generated `__claspSchemas` contract already exposes enough helpers (`fromHost`/`toHost` plus binary codecs) to build a transport benchmark without touching compiler code.
`scripts/verify-all.sh` already had a clean benchmark-test seam, so adding one more end-to-end shell check was straightforward.
The field-name surface in `.clasp` files is easy to trip over with reserved words like `route`; benchmark fixture schemas need conservative identifier choices.
follow_ups: If BM-019 or later wants a stronger publication story, fold this JSON-vs-binary report into a shared benchmark result schema or summary command instead of leaving it as a standalone JSON artifact.
Consider separating compile/setup time from measured transport execution in the output format explicitly, since the runner currently compiles once up front and then measures only projection loops.
If native generated binary codecs become benchmark-ready, mirror this benchmark through the native path so transport comparisons cover both backends.
warnings: Do not use reserved declaration words like `route` as record field names in benchmark schema fixtures; the parser rejects them.
Running the new benchmark outside `nix develop` can fail on missing `ghc`/`cabal` toolchain, just like the other compiler-backed benchmark commands.
The benchmark test writes JSON files under `benchmarks/results/backend/`; that is expected during verification and not part of the source change itself.
residual_risks: The new BM-018 benchmark compares JSON and generated binary projections in the compiled JS runtime only; it does not add a mirrored native-runtime transport comparison.
The benchmark recompiles the schema model on each run for reproducibility, so command startup cost includes one compiler invocation before measurement begins.
